string.text: This is a semi-automatic, recoil-operated, 24-round magazine-fed, large-caliber handgun with a 3-round burst fire mode and an integrated sound suppressor, and muzzle brake. The plating indicates it's chambered for "12.7?40mm", but it seems to accept .357 rounds happily, and has a considerably high rate of fire with an long effective range, being able to hit a target accurately at about fifty meters. The laser pointer module not only seems to double as a flashlight, but also seems to include some sort of electronic scope, though you have no idea how to access it.\n It bears the brand of Misriah Armory, a new company that only started presenting their first prototypes, and it while similar to their SP-5 Magnum project, this looks like a mass produced sample. Nimble simply shrugs when asked, so you wonder if this is just the Zone being the Zone.
Standard magazine for the M6C SOCOM pistol, perfectly machined to work with the firearm's 3-round burst fire mode. \n \n
